mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near zero (0.12%), the persistent zero-rate policy stance compresses the equity discount rate structurally, generating a positive drift bias in S&P 500 forward returns as long as the rate remains below 0.25% regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS<0.25% periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample average (t-test p>0.05), or rank IC stays below 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in UNRATE exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals a turning point in the labor market cycle, historically preceding sustained risk-on equity rallies over the subsequent quarter as investors reprice recession-recovery probabilities.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.2pp over a 2-month window
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=10.0 in Dec 2009 near cycle peak
failure_condition: Triggered UNRATE-decline events fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of historical occurrences, or rank IC < 0.03 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month acceleration in INDPRO and positive net job creation in PAYEMS jointly confirm a broadening industrial recovery that is not detectable from either series alone, and this joint confirmation signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month as earnings expectations are revised upward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM > 0 and PAYEMS MoM > 0 concurrently
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=100.3043 in Dec 2009 recovering from trough
failure_condition: Joint-positive INDPRO/PAYEMS months do not outperform months where only one series is positive, measured by mean 20-day forward return difference not significant at p<0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery regime, a sustained rise in HOUST above its cyclical trough signals renewed household risk appetite and construction-sector employment gains, feeding through to broad equity market strength over a quarterly horizon; this relationship is regime-specific and does not hold during late-cycle expansions where housing is supply-constrained.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises for 2 consecutive months from a multi-year low
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=574.0 in Nov 2009 near historic lows
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with depressed housing starts
failure_condition: During non-recovery regimes (e.g., late-cycle expansion), the same HOUST uptick signal produces no significant forward-return effect or a reversed sign, and the recovery-regime effect itself vanishes (p>0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year CPIAUCSL increase crossing above 2.5% signals building inflation pressure that raises market expectations of imminent Fed tightening, causing a short-term equity drawdown over the following week as discount-rate expectations reprice.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following CPI YoY crossing above 2.5% are not significantly negative relative to unconditional baseline (p>0.10) across at least two historical tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month change in PAYEMS reflects incremental labor income growth that supports near-term consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, producing a modest but persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following trading week irrespective of macro regime.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns conditioned on positive PAYEMS MoM change show rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, indicating no exploitable signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent improvement in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) jointly signals a broad-based consumer and housing demand recovery that is stronger evidence of sustainable economic expansion than either indicator alone, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM > 0 and HOUST MoM > 0 concurrently for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS+HOUST positive-momentum periods do not outperform periods with only one series positive by a statistically significant margin (p>0.10) in 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained INDPRO increase that surpasses its 6-month moving average threshold signals durable manufacturing-sector expansion, which historically leads to positive equity returns over the following quarter as earnings growth expectations for cyclical sectors are revised upward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses above its trailing 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=100.3043 in Dec 2009 rising from 2009 trough
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following INDPRO moving-average crossovers are not significantly positive relative to unconditional baseline (p>0.10) across at least two historical recovery cycles.
